Issue: Support for additional dtypes (bool and integer) in the pad_nd function due to removal of hardcoded dtype exclusion list
Decision: Remove hardcoded dtype exclusion list in pad_nd function to support more dtypes
Rationale: Previously, _pt_pad was restricted to specific dtypes, now it is used for all dtypes when the padding mode is in the allowed set, improving flexibility and compatibility with PyTorch's optimized padding
Author: skdas20
Description: pad_nd function in the MONAI library

Argument.author: coderabbitai[bot]
Argument.argument: Previously, _pt_pad was used only if the mode was in {"constant", "reflect", "edge", "replicate", "wrap", "circular"} and the tensor dtype was not in {torch.int16, torch.int64, torch.bool, torch.uint8}. Now _pt_pad is chosen whenever the mode is in that set, regardless of dtype, which removes unnecessary restrictions and allows for more flexible dtype support.

Justification: The decision involves modifying the pad_nd function to support additional dtypes (bool and integer) by removing a hardcoded exclusion list, which aligns with modeling development (specifically, algorithm/implementation adjustments to enhance flexibility). Keywords 'dtype', 'mode', and 'flexible' point toward modeling-related changes.
